fresh_facts.claim: Israel's defense minister confirmed an airstrike Monday killing IRGC intelligence chief Major General Majid Khademi, describing it as a 'major blow' to Iranian operations.
The IDF struck Iran's largest petrochemical facility in Eslamiyeh, which produces 50% of Iran's national petrochemical output, causing tens of billions in economic damage.
Four people were killed in Haifa Monday when Iranian missiles struck a residential building.
The UAE intercepted 14 Iranian missiles and 19 drones over the past 24 hours, with 4 people wounded.
Jordan confirmed it was targeted by Iranian missiles and drones in the last 24 hours, with one missile and one drone falling in Jordanian territory.
The U.S. conducted a massive rescue operation for an F-15E crew shot down in southwest Iran, involving over 150 aircraft and 200 munitions.
The IRGC vowed 'Operation Crushing Revenge' Monday via Sepah News to avenge Major General Majid Khademi's death.
President Trump threatened to 'decimate' every Iranian bridge and destroy power plants if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened by Tuesday evening.
Trump postponed energy strikes against Iran for five days after reporting 'productive' indirect talks via envoys.
Pakistan proposed a two-phase ceasefire deal to halt hostilities and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi reviewed a 15-point US plan delivered via Pakistan that includes Hormuz reopening, missile limits, sanctions relief, and nuclear rollback.
Iran rejected ceasefire proposals and insisted on no direct negotiations with the U.S.
Mojtaba Khamenei was appointed Supreme Leader after his father Ali Khamenei's death in late February 2026 in U.S.-Israel strikes.
Iran named hardliner Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr, ex-IRGC deputy, as Supreme National Security Council chief, replacing killed Ali Larijani.
Hardliners attacked ex-Foreign Minister Javad Zarif as a 'traitor' on Friday and called for his arrest along with ex-President Rouhani for advocating war cessation.
Iran executed a fourth protester Saturday over a January Basij base fire, with 18-year-old Amirhossein Hatami executed Thursday with his body subsequently withheld.
An Israeli security source claimed Iranians are 'weeks away' from another uprising, building on prior serious unrest.
UAE arrests of IRGC-linked money changers threaten Iran's shadow finance operations and Dubai trade lifeline.
U.S. strikes targeted dual-use sites including Mobarakeh and Khuzestan steel plants and transport infrastructure tied to nuclear-linked facilities.
The UN warned against attacks on Iranian civilian infrastructure following Trump's threats to strike power grids and energy sites.
Israel accelerated strikes on Tehran airports and other sites amid reports of potential war conclusion.
Houthis joined ongoing military operations, risking potential blockade of the Bab al-Mandab strait.
Approximately 2,000 additional U.S. Marines are deploying to the region as Trump's five-day pause on energy strikes ends Friday.
Trump confirmed ongoing phone talks with a 'top' Iranian official, despite denials from Iran's parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf.
Iran's military attacks on regional energy infrastructure and efforts to control the Strait of Hormuz have driven global energy prices higher.
The State Department announced a monetary reward for information on attacks against U.S. diplomatic facilities worldwide.

regime_fall.current_status: Day 40 escalation intensifies with Israeli strikes killing IRGC intelligence chief Khademi and destroying 50% of Iran's petrochemical capacity, while Iran vows 'Operation Crushing Revenge' and continues regional attacks; diplomatic signals remain contradictory with Pakistan-brokered ceasefire proposals and a 5-day US bombing pause against hardline Iranian rejection and new hardline leadership consolidation.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Israel killed IRGC intelligence chief Major General Majid Khademi and struck Iran's largest petrochemical facility (producing 50% of national output), causing tens of billions in economic damage, while the IRGC vowed 'Operation Crushing Revenge' in response.
